column1: # Closed-class English function words — the native POS tagger supplement.
# AGID's infl.txt is open-class only (V/N/A); LanguageTool's `remap.awk` deliberately omits
# determiners (its `D` rule is commented out) and mistags the rest (the→JJ, and→NN, to→JJ). LT itself
# supplies these from its hand-maintained binary dictionary, which is not in the public checkout. This
# file is the equivalent hand-authored set: the high-frequency function words that appear in nearly
# every sentence, with their LanguageTool (Penn-derived) tags — see resource/en/tagset.txt.
# These assignments are stable English grammar, not version-specific, so the file is hand-authored
# (license-clean, committed) rather than derived from LT data. `build-tagger` REPLACES the awk output
# for each surface listed here. Words may carry several analyses (the tagger stores all; P3
# disambiguation narrows to context). Format: inflected<TAB>lemma<TAB>tag — one analysis per line.
